{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Representative",
  "description": "Whip-Smart Service Providers Limited - Our client, KidNation, a leading kiddies store, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a friendly, enthusiastic, and customer-focused Customer Service Representative to provide exceptional support to our customers.
	The ideal candidate will manage customer interactions across our social media platforms, assist parents and guardians with product inquiries, process orders, and ensure a seamless shopping experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly and professionally to customer inquiries via Instagram, WhatsApp, and Facebook.
	Assist customers with product information, availability, pricing, and recommendations.
	Recommend suitable products based on customers&#39; needs and preferences.
	Confirm orders, payments, and delivery details.
	Follow up on customer inquiries and pending orders.
	Handle customer complaints, feedback, and after-sales support effectively.
	Maintain accurate customer records and update databases regularly.
	Build positive relationships with customers to encourage repeat patronage.
	Ensure a pleasant and professional customer experience at all times.
	Escalate complex issues to management when necessary.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification.
	Previous experience in customer service, retail, social media management, or a related role is an added advantage.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong customer service and relationship management skills.
	Good knowledge of Instagram, WhatsApp Business, Facebook, and other social media platforms.
	Basic computer skills and attention to detail.
	Ability to multitask and work independently.
	Friendly, patient, and professional demeanor.


Location Requirements:


	Applicants must reside in Festac or its immediate environs.
	Candidates who do not live in Festac, Amuwo Odofin, Apple Junction, Satellite Town, or nearby areas should not apply.


Salary
N100,000 &ndash; N120,000 monthly.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: whipsmartrecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Customer Service Representative &ndash; KidNation&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more enquiries, contact on WhatsApp: 09131150110.
	Applicants must reside in Festac or its immediate environs.
	Candidates who do not live in Festac, Amuwo Odofin, Apple Junction, Satellite Town, or nearby areas should not apply.

Customer Service Representative Job at Whip-Smart Service Providers Limited




        
    

        
        Whip-Smart Service Providers Limited - Our client, KidNation, a leading kiddies store, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Festac, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	Our client is seeking a friendly, enthusiastic, and customer-focused Customer Service Representative to provide exceptional support to our customers.
	The ideal candidate will manage customer interactions across our social media platforms, assist parents and guardians with product inquiries, process orders, and ensure a seamless shopping experience.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly and professionally to customer inquiries via Instagram, WhatsApp, and Facebook.
	Assist customers with product information, availability, pricing, and recommendations.
	Recommend suitable products based on customers' needs and preferences.
	Confirm orders, payments, and delivery details.
	Follow up on customer inquiries and pending orders.
	Handle customer complaints, feedback, and after-sales support effectively.
	Maintain accurate customer records and update databases regularly.
	Build positive relationships with customers to encourage repeat patronage.
	Ensure a pleasant and professional customer experience at all times.
	Escalate complex issues to management when necessary.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification.
	Previous experience in customer service, retail, social media management, or a related role is an added advantage.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Strong customer service and relationship management skills.
	Good knowledge of Instagram, WhatsApp Business, Facebook, and other social media platforms.
	Basic computer skills and attention to detail.
	Ability to multitask and work independently.
	Friendly, patient, and professional demeanor.


Location Requirements:


	Applicants must reside in Festac or its immediate environs.
	Candidates who do not live in Festac, Amuwo Odofin, Apple Junction, Satellite Town, or nearby areas should not apply.


Salary
N100,000 – N120,000 monthly. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Customer Service Representative – KidNation" as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more enquiries, contact on WhatsApp: 09131150110.
	Applicants must reside in Festac or its immediate environs.
	Candidates who do not live in Festac, Amuwo Odofin, Apple Junction, Satellite Town, or nearby areas should not apply.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Audit",
  "description": "Whip-Smart Service Providers Limited is an HR Recruiting Firm, bridging the gap between job seekers and employers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Audit
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and strategic Head of Audit to lead and oversee the organization&#39;s internal audit function.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and implementing audit strategies, evaluating internal controls, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, identifying business risks, and providing recommendations to strengthen governance and operational efficiency.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong leadership skills, extensive audit experience, and a deep understanding of risk management, internal controls, corporate governance, and financial reporting.


Key Responsibilities
Audit Leadership &amp; Strategy:


	Develop and implement the organization&#39;s annual audit plan based on risk assessments.
	Lead and manage the internal audit function to ensure effective execution of audit activities.
	Establish audit policies, procedures, and methodologies in line with industry best practices.
	Provide strategic guidance on risk management and internal control frameworks.


Internal Control &amp; Risk Management:


	Evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls across business operations.
	Identify operational, financial, compliance, and strategic risks.
	Recommend practical solutions to mitigate identified risks and strengthen control processes.
	Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and corrective actions.


Financial &amp; Operational Audits:


	Conduct comprehensive audits of financial records, business processes, and operational activities.
	Review accounting systems and financial reporting processes to ensure accuracy and compliance.
	Assess the efficiency and effectiveness of business operations and resource utilization.
	Investigate irregularities, control weaknesses, and potential fraud risks.


Compliance &amp; Governance:


	Ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations, and corporate policies.
	Review adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards.
	Support corporate governance initiatives and promote ethical business practices.
	Liaise with regulatory bodies and external auditors where necessary.


Reporting &amp; Stakeholder Management:


	Prepare and present audit findings, reports, and recommendations to executive management and the Board.
	Provide regular updates on audit activities, risk exposures, and control improvements.
	Collaborate with department heads to implement corrective actions and process improvements.
	Serve as a trusted advisor to management on audit and risk-related matters.


Team Leadership:


	Lead, mentor, and develop the internal audit team.
	Allocate audit assignments and monitor performance.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement within the organization.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, ICAN, CIA, CISA, or equivalent is required.
	Minimum of 8--10years of progressive audit experience, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role.
	Strong knowledge of internal auditing standards, risk management frameworks, and corporate governance principles.
	Experience in financial audits, operational audits, compliance reviews, and fraud investigations.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Strong leadership and people management abilities.
	Exceptional report writing and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in audit tools, ERP systems, and Microsoft Office applications.


Key Competencies:


	Strategic Thinking
	Internal Audit Management
	Risk Assessment and Mitigation
	Corporate Governance
	Financial Analysis
	Compliance Management
	Leadership and Team Development
	Stakeholder Management
	Investigation and Fraud Detection
	Communication and Presentation Skills.


Saalry &amp; Benefits


	N1,500,000 &ndash; N2,000,000 Monthly.
	Competitive Executive Compensation Package
	Professional Development Opportunities
	Leadership Role with Strategic Impact
	Collaborative and High-Performance Work Environment


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers4@whip-smart.com.ng using &quot;Head of Audit &ndash; Ikeja&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates who meet the requirements will be contacted.",

Head of Audit Job at Whip-Smart Service




        
    

        
        Whip-Smart Service Providers Limited is an HR Recruiting Firm, bridging the gap between job seekers and employers.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Audit
Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and strategic Head of Audit to lead and oversee the organization's internal audit function.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and implementing audit strategies, evaluating internal controls, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements, identifying business risks, and providing recommendations to strengthen governance and operational efficiency.
	The ideal candidate must possess strong leadership skills, extensive audit experience, and a deep understanding of risk management, internal controls, corporate governance, and financial reporting.


Key Responsibilities
Audit Leadership & Strategy:


	Develop and implement the organization's annual audit plan based on risk assessments.
	Lead and manage the internal audit function to ensure effective execution of audit activities.
	Establish audit policies, procedures, and methodologies in line with industry best practices.
	Provide strategic guidance on risk management and internal control frameworks.


Internal Control & Risk Management:


	Evaluate the effectiveness of internal controls across business operations.
	Identify operational, financial, compliance, and strategic risks.
	Recommend practical solutions to mitigate identified risks and strengthen control processes.
	Monitor the implementation of audit recommendations and corrective actions.


Financial & Operational Audits:


	Conduct comprehensive audits of financial records, business processes, and operational activities.
	Review accounting systems and financial reporting processes to ensure accuracy and compliance.
	Assess the efficiency and effectiveness of business operations and resource utilization.
	Investigate irregularities, control weaknesses, and potential fraud risks.


Compliance & Governance:


	Ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations, and corporate policies.
	Review adherence to regulatory requirements and industry standards.
	Support corporate governance initiatives and promote ethical business practices.
	Liaise with regulatory bodies and external auditors where necessary.


Reporting & Stakeholder Management:


	Prepare and present audit findings, reports, and recommendations to executive management and the Board.
	Provide regular updates on audit activities, risk exposures, and control improvements.
	Collaborate with department heads to implement corrective actions and process improvements.
	Serve as a trusted advisor to management on audit and risk-related matters.


Team Leadership:


	Lead, mentor, and develop the internal audit team.
	Allocate audit assignments and monitor performance.
	Promote a culture of accountability, compliance, and continuous improvement within the organization.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certification such as ACA, ACCA, ICAN, CIA, CISA, or equivalent is required.
	Minimum of 8--10years of progressive audit experience, with at least 5 years in a senior leadership role.
	Strong knowledge of internal auditing standards, risk management frameworks, and corporate governance principles.
	Experience in financial audits, operational audits, compliance reviews, and fraud investigations.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
	Strong leadership and people management abilities.
	Exceptional report writing and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in audit tools, ERP systems, and Microsoft Office applications.


Key Competencies:


	Strategic Thinking
	Internal Audit Management
	Risk Assessment and Mitigation
	Corporate Governance
	Financial Analysis
	Compliance Management
	Leadership and Team Development
	Stakeholder Management
	Investigation and Fraud Detection
	Communication and Presentation Skills.


Saalry & Benefits


	N1,500,000 – N2,000,000 Monthly.
	Competitive Executive Compensation Package
	Professional Development Opportunities
	Leadership Role with Strategic Impact
	Collaborative and High-Performance Work Environment


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Head of Audit – Ikeja" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates who meet the requirements will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Aureole Consulting Limited
content:
Aureole Consulting Limited is an HR consulting company which specialize in talent acquisition services, Outsourcing Services, Executive Search, Social Media Job Advertisement, HR consulting amongst other services. Our aim is to revolutionize HR consulting in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Business Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Food & Hospitality

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Business Manager to oversee the day-to-day operations of our food and hospitality business, ensuring operational efficiency, profitability, and exceptional customer satisfaction
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving business growth, managing budgets and operational costs, leading high-performing teams, and identifying opportunities to improve performance and expand market presence.


Job Responsibilities


	Oversee daily business operations to ensure efficiency and profitability.
	Develop and implement growth strategies to increase revenue and business performance.
	Manage business budgets, expenses, and operational costs.
	Monitor staff performance and ensure operational excellence across departments.
	Build and maintain strong customer and stakeholder relationships.
	Analyze market trends and identify opportunities for business expansion.
	Ensure excellent customer experience and service delivery.
	Prepare reports and provide strategic recommendations to management.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Marketing, or a related field.
	3 - 5 years of proven experience in a managerial or business management role, preferably in the food, hospitality, or service industry.
	Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent business development and operational management abilities.
	Financial management and reporting skills.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities.


Salary
N300,000 – N400,000 Monthly (Net)

Application Closing Date: 1st July, 2026.

Method of Appllication 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the mail.

Note: Preferably male candidates (for gender balance).

 

2.) B2B Sales Representative

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Identify & Develop: Discover new B2B sales opportunities to expand market reach.
	Relationship Management: Build and maintain strong, long-term relationships with corporate clients and partners.
	Present & Negotiate: Pitch products/services to prospective clients and confidently negotiate contracts.
	Target Driven: Meet and exceed assigned monthly/quarterly sales targets.
	Market Insights: Conduct market research to identify potential customers and stay ahead of industry trends.
	Data Tracking: Maintain accurate, up-to-date records of sales activities and client interactions.
	Collaboration: Work closely with internal teams to ensure smooth onboarding and excellent service delivery.


Requirements


	Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field (preferred).
	Experience: Proven track record in B2B or corporate sales.
	Core Skills: Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Attributes: Ability to work independently, self-motivated, and target-driven.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, etc.).
	Networking: Strong relationship-building abilities within corporate environments.


Salary & Benefits


	Salary: N128,487.84 per month
	Benefits: Leave Allowance | Pension | HMO | 13th Month Salary


Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

3.) Accountant Intern (NYSC)

Job Location:  Lagos (Remote)
Employment Type: Nysc

Job Summary


	We are looking for an Accountant Intern to join our team remotely.
	This opportunity is strictly open to Corp Members who have recently commenced their service year or just completed orientation camp.


Requirements


	B.Sc. Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field
	Currently serving (NYSC) or recently completed orientation camp
	Basic accounting knowledge
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
	Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
	Must reside within Lagos (Remote)


Salary
N50,000 - N60,000 monthly

Application Closing Date: 8th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at First Bank of Nigeria Limited
content:
First Bank of Nigeria Limited (FirstBank) is Nigeria's largest financial services institution by total assets and gross earnings. With more than 10 million customer accounts, FirstBank has over 750 branches providing a comprehensive range of retail and corporate financial services. The Bank has international presence through its subsidiaries, FBN Bank (UK) Limited in London and Paris, FBNBank DRC, FBNBank Ghana, FBNBank Gambia, FBNBank Guinea, FBNBank Sierra-Leone and FBNBank Senegal, as well as its Representative Offices in Johannesburg, Beijing and Abu Dhabi.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Retail Banking Recruitment Bootcamp - Relationship Manager (North Division)
     

            
    



Job Identification: 1787
Job Location: Nigeria
Job Schedule: Full time
Grade: ABO – SBO

Job Objective


	To effectively sell the bank’s products, mobilize and grow deposits liabilities through the various products and generate revenues. Also, source for new customers and maintain existing accounts.


Duties & Responsibilities


	Book quality assets and ensure effective portfolio monitoring for early detection of defaults.
	Manage and deepen relationships with existing and prospective customers.
	Win new accounts and resuscitate dormant relationships.
	Market the bank’s products to meet customer needs.
	Initiate development of products required to meet customer needs.
	Ensure efficient service and maintenance of existing customer.
	Ensure zero tolerance for inactive/dormant accounts.
	Perform all other duties as assigned by the Business Manager.


Requirements
Education:


	Minimum Education: First Degree or its equivalent in any discipline preferably business-related.
	Higher Degrees (MBA)/Professional Certificate will be an added advantage.


 Experience:


	Minimum experience – 3 years of relevant banking (marketing and credit) experience


Knowledge:


	Financial advisory
	Good Knowledge of the business environment
	Credit/Risk Management
	Banking structure, policies, and procedures
	Banking Operations, Products & Services
	Customer Service
	Marketing/ Sales 
	Business/ Product Development
	Budget Planning & Control  
	Financial Analysis Business/ Operational Strategy
	Strategic Business Planning


Application Closing Date: 6th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

Note: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

 

2.) Retail Banking Recruitment Bootcamp - Relationship Manager

Job Identification: 1786
Job Location: Lagos, South-West, South-East, South-South
Job Schedule: Full time
Grade: ABO – SBO

Job Objective


	Responsible for developing the business unit to increase sales, profitability, market share and customer satisfaction.


Duties & Responsibilities 


	Supervise activities of retail marketing staff to ensure timely and efficient service delivery to customers. 
	Promote the sales and service culture through coaching, guidance and staff motivation 
	Ensure achievement of monthly and annual targets 
	Approve expenditure for the branch marketing team within limits 
	Ensure branch operating cost is within approved budget 
	Ensure zero tolerance for inactive/dormant accounts 
	Manage all staff in the team to achieve increased value creation, volume of business and enhanced profitability for the bank 
	Ensure effective risk asset appraisal, disbursement and monitoring 
	Provide timely and accurate reports in prescribed formats and intervals 
	Ensure excellent working relationship with the branch operations staff 
	Perform all other duties as assigned by the Group Head.


Requirements
Education:


	Minimum Education: First Degree or its equivalent in any discipline preferably business-related.
	Higher Degrees (MBA)/Professional Certificate will be an added advantage


 Experience:


	Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience and at least 2 years in management position with responsibility in Retail & marketing units, relationship management, product management and Marketing and retail sales.


Knowledge:


	Financial advisory
	Good Knowledge of the business environment
	Credit/Risk Management
	Banking structure, policies, and procedures
	Banking Operations, Products & Services
	Customer Service
	Marketing/ Sales 
	Business/ Product Development
	Budget Planning & Control  
	Financial Analysis Business/Operational Strategy
	Strategic Business Planning.


Application Closing Date: 6th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

Note: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted. 

 

3.) Analyst, Subsidiary Finance & Capital Optimisation

Job Identification: 1785
Job Location: Lagos
Job Schedule: Full time

Duties & Responsibilities


	Support coordination of subsidiary budgeting process
	Support timely preparation and rendition of financial reports by subsidiaries for management reporting purposes
	Support timely rendition of appropriate statutory reports to regulators
	Support preparation of Group Management Committee (GMC) reporting and Monthly Performance Reviews (MPRs) regarding subsidiary performance and insights
	Support preparation for and coordination of Monthly Performance Reviews (MPRs)
	Support timely preparation and submission of relevant board and management reports
	Contribute to process improvements to drive quality and timely reporting of the team
	Assist in coordinating interim and statutory audit processes of subsidiaries
	Assist in ensuring monthly and quarterly subsidiary performance review reports are presented to the Management Committee (MANCO) and Board of Directors
	Assist in coordinating all corporate finance activities for the Group, including mergers and acquisitions, divestments, capital raising, restructurings, equity and debt financing
	Assist in preparing Request for Proposals (RFPs) for advisors for corporate finance transactions
	Assist in selection and shortlisting of transaction and legal advisers for corporate finance transactions
	Assist in preparing financial models and work with transaction advisers to predict outcomes and support decision-making
	Assist in monitoring and project managing transaction workplans to ensure all target deadlines are met
	Assist in contributing to competitive bid proposals, formal presentations, and other documentation
	Assist in preparing information memoranda, valuation reports, and investor presentations
	Assist in monitoring due diligence processes conducted by advisers and providing regular updates to management
	Assist in obtaining relevant regulatory and internal approvals to ensure closure of corporate finance transactions
	Assist in end-to-end management of capital across all group entities, including ICAAP
	Assist in ensuring compliance with relevant policies
	Assist in ensuring policies and Service Level Agreements (SLAs) are up to date and reviewed as required
	Assist in ensuring adherence to SLAs and agreements with vendors
	Assist in the Bank’s credit rating process to achieve targeted ratings
	Carry out any other duties as assigned by the HOD or HOU


Requirements


	Education:  First degree in preferably numerate discipline. CFA, ACA, ACCA, CPA, CMA, MBA is an added advantage.
	Experience: Experience in corporate finance, Investment banking and capital management in financial services or related industry.


Application Closing Date: 25th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply
